sections.title: A great system invites play beyond the win condition
sections.summary: Wright's body of work consistently weakens the distinction between toy and game. The important product lesson is not that goals disappear. It is that the possibility space remains

sections.title: Emergence depends on legible building blocks
sections.summary: Wright's games feel open, but they are not shapeless. Emergence works because the base rules are understandable enough that players can form hypotheses and enjoy surprises. For Tem

sections.title: Simulation can change how players think

sections.title: Creativity needs leverage, not just freedom

sections.title: Player-authored stories are a product asset

sections.title: What changed over time
sections.summary: The underlying interest in simulation and creativity is steady. Later interviews widen the scope beyond games into entertainment systems more generally, but the core pattern stays

sections.title: Questions this hat asks

sections.title: Runtime notes
sections.summary: Default message style: - curious - systems-oriented - exploratory - generative Good output shape: - name the system interaction at stake - point out whether it increases or narrows

sections.title: TED speaker framing
sections.summary: Useful for Wright's emphasis on learning, invention, and abstract systems made playful.
